---
title: 'Analysis Plan'
format:
  html:
    css: styles.css
    embed-resources: TRUE
    code-fold: true
    page-layout: full
    fig_caption: yes
    toc: TRUE
---

```{=html}
<style>
  table {
    width: 100%;
    border-collapse: collapse;
  }
  th, td {
    border: 1px solid #ddd;
    padding: 8px;
  }
  th {
    background-color: #f2f2f2;
    text-align: left;
  }
  tr:nth-child(even) {
    background-color: #f9f9f9;
  }
  tr:hover {
    background-color: #d0cece;
  }

  /* Style for the second column */
  th:nth-child(2), td:nth-child(2) {
    font-size: smaller; /* Adjusts the font size */
    color: gray; /* Changes the text color to gray */
  }

  /* Class for red text */
  .red-text {
    color: #E25448 !important;
  }
</style>
```
```{=html}
<script>
  document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", function() {
    var cells = document.querySelectorAll("td:nth-child(2)");
    cells.forEach(function(cell) {
      if (cell.textContent.includes("Policy Team/Stakeholder Data Needed")) {
        cell.classList.add("red-text");
      }
    });
  });
</script>
```
This analysis plan outlines research questions and methodology for understanding various aspects of West Virginia's criminal justice system, accounting for the data limitations provided by the DCR.

## Costs

This section evaluates the financial implications of incarceration. By analyzing average costs for jails, prisons, probation, and parole, and assessing the impact of potential reductions in the jail population or changes in parole policies, we aim to identify areas for cost savings and more efficient resource allocation.

| Research Questions                                                                                                                      | Data Sources                                      |
|------------------------------------------|------------------------------|
| What are the average costs per person per day for jails and prisons?                                                                    | Sources: DCR Jail/DOC Data; Avg Cost Per Day      |
| How do costs vary with changes in facility populations?                                                                                 | Sources: DCR Jail/DOC Data; Avg Cost Per Day      |
| What are the average costs per person per day for probation and parole supervision?                                                     | Sources: DCR DOC Data; Avg Cost Per Day           |
| How would a 10% reduction in the jail population impact overall costs? Reduction in pretrial detention?                                 | Sources: DCR Jail Data; Marginal Cost Per Day     |
| What cost savings can be achieved by granting parole on the eligibility date?                                                           | Sources: DCR DOC Data; Marginal Cost Per Day      |
| How does the cost of incarcerating people for non-violent crimes compare to the cost of supervising them in the community?              | Sources: DCR Jail/DOC Data; Avg Cost Per Day      |
| What are the projected cost savings from reducing technical violations and revocations for probation and parole?                        | Sources: DCR Jail/DOC Data; Marginal Cost Per Day |
| What are the costs associated with re-incarceration within one year of release for different offense types?                             | Sources: DCR Jail/DOC Data; Avg Cost Per Day      |
| How much would it cost to actually implement a program in the state?                                                                    | Policy Team/Stakeholder Data Needed               |
| How do the costs of BH services in community settings compare to those provided in jail or prison?                                      | Policy Team/Stakeholder Data Needed               |
| What are the financial implications of providing healthcare to an aging prison population in WV?                                        | Policy Team/Stakeholder Data Needed               |
| How does the cost of caring for older people compare to younger people in terms of medical care, housing, and other resources?          | Policy Team/Stakeholder Data Needed               |



## Jails

In this section, we will investigate the demographics, offenses, and lengths of stay in jails, alongside the prevalence of substance use and mental health conditions. This analysis will provide insights into who is being incarcerated, the reasons behind their admissions, and the common patterns of re-incarceration. Understanding these factors will help in identifying trends and issues within the jail system, and inform strategies to address large jail populations, improve conditions, and support targeted interventions for individuals with specific needs.

| Research Questions                                                                                                                                        | Data Sources                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
|------------------------------------------|------------------------------|
| What are the demographic characteristics (age, gender, race, ethnicity) of the jail population?                                                           | DOB, gender, Race, Ethnicity <br> Sources: DCR Jail Data - Admissions, Releases                                                                                                                                 |
| What are the rates of jail admissions for individuals with prior incarcerations?                                                                          | Admission Date, Release Date <br> Sources: DCR Jail Data - Admissions, Releases                                                                                                                                 |
| What are the average and median lengths of stay in jails? By offense?                                                                                     | Admission Date, Release Date <br> Sources: DCR Jail Data - Admissions, Releases                                                                                                                                 |
| What are the most common offenses leading to jail admissions?                                                                                             | Controlling Offense Code, Controlling Offense Description, Offense Type, Offense Severity/Degree <br> Sources: DCR Jail Data - Admissions                                                                       |
| What are the pretrial detention rates across jails? And county? North Central characteristics? Remapping to reduce pressure on North Central?             | Type of Admission <br> Sources: DCR - Jail Data                                                                                                                                                                 |
| What are capacities at each jail (not all in annual reports)? How overcapacity are the jails and how has that changed over time?                          | Type of Admission <br> Sources: DCR - Jail Data, Policy Team/Stakeholder Data Needed                                                                                                                            |
| What proportion of jail admissions are for technical parole/probation violations?                                                                         | Type of Admission, Type of Release <br> Sources: DCR Jail Data - Admissions, Releases                                                                                                                           |
| How many incarcerated individuals in jails have substance use disorders or mental health conditions?                                                      | Substance Abuse (SA) Screening/Assessment Scores, Known History of Drug Alcohol Abuse, Mental Health (MH) Screening/Assessment Scores, Known History of Mental Illness <br> Sources: DCR Jail Data - Admissions |
| What is the average age and gender distribution of individuals admitted to jails?                                                                         | DOB, gender, Race, Ethnicity <br> Sources: DCR Jail Data - Admissions, Releases                                                                                                                                 |
| What are the most common release types from jails (e.g., bail, release on own recognizance, completion of sentence)?                                      | Type of Release <br> Sources: DCR Jail Data - Releases                                                                                                                                                          |
| What are the most common release types by LOS? By offense?                                                                                                | Type of Release, Offense Description, Admission Date, Release Date <br> Sources: DCR Jail Data - Admissions, Releases                                                                                           |
| How often do individuals return to jail after release, and what are the common reasons for re-admission?                                                  | Admission Date, Release Date, Type of Admission, Type of Release <br> Sources: DCR Jail Data - Admissions, Releases                                                                                             |
| How does the county of arrest influence jail admissions and lengths of stay?                                                                              | County of Arrest <br> Sources: DCR Jail Data - Admissions, Releases                                                                                                                                             |
| Which jails have higher rates of entrances by people who are "familiar faces", or the top 10% of people who cycle in and out of regional jail facilities? | DCR Jail Data - Admissions                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| How do the lengths of stay and re-incarceration rates compare between individuals with and without behavioral health conditions?                          | Substance Abuse (SA) Screening/Assessment Scores, Known History of Drug Alcohol Abuse, Mental Health (MH) Screening/Assessment Scores, Known History of Mental Illness <br> Sources: DCR Jail Data - Admissions |

## Staffing

This section examines the current staffing levels and challenges faced within the criminal justice system. It highlights key aspects such as recruitment, retention, and staffing ratios.

| Research Questions                                                                                                                                                                                | Data Sources                                                                                                                                |
|------------------------------------------|------------------------------|
| The current incarcerated population and staffing levels (include work hours and overtime), ideal staffing ratios, the required reduction in population numbers to meet these ratios? By facility? | Sources: Policy Team/Stakeholder Data Needed                                                                                                |
| What are the turnover rates for correctional officers and staff?                                                                                                                                  | Sources: Policy Team/Stakeholder Data Needed                                                                                                |
| How do staffing levels and capacity by facility compare in terms of incidents of violence or misconduct in facilities?                                                                            | Incident Reports, Disciplinary Records <br> Sources: DCR Data - Incident Reports, Disciplinary Records, Policy Team/Stakeholder Data Needed |
| How has the staffing shortage impacted the daily operations in these facilities?                                                                                                                  | Policy Team/Stakeholder Data Needed                                                                                                         |
| How do salaries for similar positions compare across neighboring states?                                                                                                                          | Policy Team/Stakeholder Data Needed                                                                                                         |
| What percentage of the current jail population exceeds the capacity that can be safely managed by the current staffing levels?                                                                    | Policy Team/Stakeholder Data Needed                                                                                                         |
| What are the potential short-term and long-term strategies to address staffing shortages (e.g., recruitment drives, better compensation, improved working conditions)?                            | Policy Team/Stakeholder Data Needed                                                                                                         |
| What are the potential strategies to reduce the population to manageable levels?                                                                                                                  | Policy Team/Stakeholder Data Needed                                                                                                         |
| Circumstances and duration of National Guard involvement, including its impact on jail and prison operations?                                                                                     | Policy Team/Stakeholder Data Needed                                                                                                         |

## Cost Analysis Based on Illinois Study

[Study Link](https://spac.illinois.gov/publications/fiscal-impact-analysis/dynamic-marginal-costs-fiscal-impacts-2018)

**Dynamic Marginal Costs Definition:** These costs estimate the financial impact of adding or removing people in the criminal justice system. They include variable costs (directly related to services like food and laundry), step costs (personnel costs), and fixed costs (costs related to physical spaces).

### Variable Costs (VC)
These costs change directly with the number of incarcerated people. Examples include food, laundry, and medical care. 

**Formula:** `VC = Average cost per individual for food, medical care, etc.`

**Example:**

- If food and medical care cost $10,000 per individual per year:
  `VC = 10,000`

### Step Costs (SC)
These costs change when the number of incarcerated people hits certain levels or "thresholds." For example, hiring more staff when the prison population increases by a certain number. 

1. Identify the threshold (T) at which new staff or resources are needed.
2. Calculate the total step cost (TC_SC) for reaching the threshold.
3. Determine the per-individual step cost:

**Formula:** `SC = TC_SC / T`

**Example:**

- For every 50 additional individuals, 5 new staff are hired at $50,000 per year each.
- Total step cost for 50 individuals:
  `TC_SC = 5 * 50,000 = 250,000`
- Per-individual step cost:
  `SC = 250,000 / 50 = 5,000`

### Fixed Costs (FC)
These costs don't change with small changes in the prison population but do change with large population shifts. Examples include the cost of the building and administrative overhead.

1. Identify the large change threshold (L) that triggers fixed costs.
2. Calculate the total fixed cost (TC_FC) for reaching this large change threshold.
3. Determine the per-individual fixed cost:

**Formula:** `FC = TC_FC / L`

**Example:**

- Building a new facility wing costs $500,000 every 200 individuals.
- Total fixed cost for 200 individuals:
  `TC_FC = 500,000`
- Per-individual fixed cost:
  `FC = 500,000 / 200 = 2,500`

### Data Needed
1. **Historical Staffing Data:** Information on staff changes with prison population changes.
2. **Personnel Costs:** Detailed costs for staff salaries, benefits, and pensions.
3. **Operational Data:** Records of costs related to running the correctional facilities.
4. **Capital Expenditure Data:** Costs for building and maintaining prison facilities.
5. **Administrative Overhead Costs:** Costs for running administrative offices.
6. **Debt and Interest Payments:** Data on loans or bonds for facility construction or upgrades.
7. **Budget Reports:** Detailed budget reports from the WV Department of Corrections and county jails.
8. **Thresholds for Staffing Changes:** Specific thresholds at which staffing levels change.

### Small Changes (Variable Costs Only)
**Formula:** `DMC = VC * ΔN`

**Example:**

- If adding 10 individuals:
  - Variable cost per individual (VC): $10,000
  - Change in number of individuals (ΔN): 10
  - Calculation:
    `DMC = VC * ΔN`
    `DMC = 10,000 * 10 = 100,000`
  - Total Cost Impact: $100,000

### Medium Changes (Variable + Step Costs)
**Formula:** `DMC = (VC + SC) * ΔN`

**Example:**

- If adding 50 individuals:
  - Variable cost per individual (VC): $10,000
  - Step cost per individual (SC): $5,000
  - Change in number of individuals (ΔN): 50
  - Calculation:
    `DMC = (VC + SC) * ΔN`
    `DMC = (10,000 + 5,000) * 50 = 750,000`
  - Total Cost Impact: $750,000

### Large Changes (Variable + Step + Fixed Costs)
**Formula:** `DMC = (VC + SC + FC) * ΔN`

**Example:**

- If adding 200 individuals:
  - Variable cost per individual (VC): $10,000
  - Step cost per individual (SC): $5,000
  - Fixed cost per individual (FC): $2,500
  - Change in number of individuals (ΔN): 200
  - Calculation:
    `DMC = (VC + SC + FC) * ΔN`
    `DMC = (10,000 + 5,000 + 2,500) * 200 = 3,500,000`
  - Total Cost Impact: $3,500,000

### Cost Savings

#### Small Changes (Variable Costs Only)

**Example:** 

- If a policy reduces the prison population by 100 individuals:
  - Reduction in variable costs (VC): $10,000 per individual
  - Cost Savings:
    `Savings = VC * ΔN`
    `Savings = 10,000 * 100 = 1,000,000`
  - Total Savings: $1,000,000

#### Medium Changes (Variable + Step Costs)

**Example:**

- If a policy reduces the prison population by 50 individuals:
  - Variable cost per individual (VC): $10,000
  - Step cost per individual (SC): $5,000
  - Cost Savings:
    `Savings = (VC + SC) * ΔN`
    `Savings = (10,000 + 5,000) * 50 = 750,000`
  - Total Savings: $750,000

#### Large Changes (Variable + Step + Fixed Costs)

**Example:**

- If a policy reduces the prison population by 200 individuals:
  - Variable cost per individual (VC): $10,000
  - Step cost per individual (SC): $5,000
  - Fixed cost per individual (FC): $2,500
  - Cost Savings:
    `Savings = (VC + SC + FC) * ΔN`
    `Savings = (10,000 + 5,000 + 2,500) * 200 = 3,500,000`
  - Total Savings: $3,500,000
 




## Aging/Second Look

[WAIT FOR LEGISLATURE BACKING - CHANGE FRAMING IF NOT SECOND LOOK]
The West Virginia Legislature has introduced two bills, [House Bill 4021](https://www.wvlegislature.gov/Bill_Status/bills_history.cfm?INPUT=4021&year=2024&sessiontype=RS) and [Senate Bill 736](https://www.wvlegislature.gov/Bill_Status/bills_history.cfm?INPUT=736&year=2024&sessiontype=RS), to establish the Second Look Sentencing Act. This legislation aims to allow individuals who have served at least 10 years of their prison sentence to apply for a sentence modification. Specifically, HB 4021 addresses all individuals who have served 10 years, whereas SB 736 focuses on those who committed their offense before the age of 25.

| Research Questions                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | Data Sources                                                                                                                  |
|------------------------------------------|------------------------------|
| How many individuals have served at least 10 years of their sentence? Among those who have served 10 years, how many were under the age of 25 at the time of their offense?                                                                       | Admission Date <br> Sources: DCR Prison Data                                                                                  |
| What were the offenses for people who have served at least 10 years/under the age of 25 at the time of their offense?                                                                                                                             | Admission Date, Offense Description <br> Sources: DCR Prison Data                                                             |
| What are the potential cost savings from reducing the population through second-look initiatives, considering reduced staffing needs and lower operational costs? What characteristics make someone eligible or a good candidate for second look? | Policy Team/Stakeholder Data Needed                                                                                           |

## Bail/Pretrial

This section examines the implications of bail and pretrial detention practices on individuals and the justice system. We aim to identify how bail decisions and pretrial detention impact recidivism, incarceration rates, and the overall fairness of the system.

| Research Questions                                                                                | Data Sources                                                                                                      |
|------------------------------------------|------------------------------|
| What is the average bail amount set for different offense types?                                     | Bail Amount, Offense Type <br> Sources: AOC Data                                                                  |
| How does the bail amount influence pretrial detention rates?                                         | Bail Amount, Pretrial Detention Status <br> Sources: AOC Data                                                     |
| What proportion of individuals are detained pretrial and what are their offense types?               | Type of Admission, Controlling Offense Code <br> Sources: DCR Jail Data - Admissions                              |
| How do bail amounts and pretrial detention rates vary across different demographic groups? facility? | Bail Amount, Type of Admission, Type of Release <br> Sources: AOC Data                                            |
| What are the demographic characteristics of individuals who are unable to afford bail?               | Bail Amount, Demographic Information <br> Sources: AOC Data                                                       |
| How do pretrial detention practices affect the length of stay in jails?                              | Pretrial Detention Status, Length of Stay <br> Sources: AOC Data, DCR Jail Data - Admissions, Releases            |
| What is the average cost of pretrial detention for different offense types?                          | Pretrial Detention Status, Offense Type, Cost Data <br> Sources: AOC Data, DOC Provided Cost Per Day              |

## Sentencing

This section focuses on understanding sentencing practices, including the length of sentences, the types of sentences imposed, and the factors influencing sentencing decisions. The goal is to analyze how sentencing patterns affect incarceration rates and explore any disparities present in sentencing.

| Research Questions                                                                                         | Data Sources                                                                                                     |
|------------------------------------------|------------------------------|
| What are the average and median lengths of sentences for different offense types?                          | Sentence Length, Offense Type <br> Sources: Sources: AOC Data                                                    |
| How do sentencing lengths vary by demographic factors (age, gender, race, ethnicity)?                      | Sentence Length, Demographic Information <br> Sources: AOC Data                                                  |
| How do sentencing patterns differ across counties or regions?                                              | Sentence Length, County/Region <br> Sources: AOC Data                                                            |
| What are the most common offenses leading to prison sentences?                                             | Offense Type, Sentence Length <br> Sources: AOC Data                                                             |
| Any research questions on indeterminate and determinate sentences?                                         | Sentence Type (Indeterminate, Determinate), Sentence Length <br> Sources: DCR Prison Data - Admissions, AOC Data |
 
**Definitions:**

-   **Indeterminate Sentences:** Sentences that include a minimum and maximum term specified in statute and cannot be varied by the court when imposing a prison sentence. Examples include:
    -   Manufacture Sch. I: 1 to 15 years
    -   Manufacture Sch. IV: 1 to 3 years
    -   Theft over \$1000: 1 to 10 years
    -   Sex Assault 3rd Degree: 1 to 5 years
    -   Sex Assault 2nd Degree: 10 to 25 years
    -   Bank Robbery: 10 to 20 years
-   **Determinate Sentences:** Sentences where the court specifies a maximum term within an authorized statutory range, but no minimum term. Definite sentences include terms to parole release eligibility that are set by statutory formula rather than the judicial sentence. By default, definite sentences carry parole-release eligibility after 25 percent of the judicial maximum term has been served. Example:
    -   First Degree Robbery: "shall be imprisoned in a state correctional facility not less than ten years."

## Prisons

The prison analysis focuses on the demographic characteristics of the prison population, common offenses, lengths of stay, and participation in programs. By examining these aspects, we aim to understand the profile of those incarcerated, the effectiveness of rehabilitation programs, and the impact of parole eligibility on prison populations.

| Research Questions                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | Data Sources                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
|------------------------------------------|------------------------------|
| What are the demographic characteristics (age, gender, race, ethnicity) of the prison population?                                                                                                                                                         | DOB, gender, Race, Ethnicity <br> Sources: DCR Prison Data - Admissions, Releases                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| What are the most common offenses leading to prison admissions?                                                                                                                                                                                           | Controlling Offense Code, Controlling Offense Description, Offense Type, Offense Severity/Degree <br> Sources: DCR Prison Data - Admissions                                                                                                                          |
| What are the average and median lengths of stay in prison for different offense types?                                                                                                                                                                    | Admission Date, Release Date <br> Sources: DCR Prison Data - Admissions, Releases                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| How many incarcerated individuals in prisons have substance use disorders or mental health conditions?                                                                                                                                                    | Substance Abuse (SA) Screening/Assessment Scores, Known History of Drug Alcohol Abuse, Mental Health (MH) Screening/Assessment Scores, Known History of Mental Illness <br> Sources: DCR Prison Data - Admissions, Assessments                                       |
| What are the most common release types from prisons (e.g., parole, completion of sentence)?                                                                                                                                                               | Type of Release <br> Sources: DCR Prison Data - Releases                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| How many people died by facility and year? Do these facilities have distinct characteristics like lack of AC?                                                                                                                                             | Type of Release <br> Sources: DCR Prison Data - Releases, Policy Team/Stakeholder Data Needed                                                                                                                                                                        |
| How many incarcerated individuals participate in educational or vocational programs in prison?                                                                                                                                                            | Program Name, Program Type, Program Referral Date, Program Start Date, Program Termination/End Date, Type of Completion (successful, incomplete, removed, transferred to another facility, etc.) <br> Sources: DCR Prison Data - Program Referrals and Participation |
| What are the patterns of disciplinary actions in prisons?                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Incident Reports, Disciplinary Actions Taken <br> Sources: DCR Prison Data - Disciplinary Records                                                                                                                                                                    |
| What is the average time to parole eligibility for different offense types?                                                                                                                                                                               | Parole Eligibility Date <br> Sources: DCR Prison Data - Admissions, Releases                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| What are the most common reasons for parole denials?                                                                                                                                                                                                      | Parole Hearing Outcome <br> Sources: TBD Parole Board Decisions Data                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| How do parole eligibility dates impact prison population sizes? In other words, how long are people kept in prison past their parole eligibility date?                                                                                                    | Admission Date, Release Date, Parole Eligibility Date <br> Sources: DCR Prison Data - Admissions, Releases                                                                                                                                                           |
| Is there a difference in the age distribution of individuals incarcerated for violent offenses compared to non-violent offenses?                                                                                                                          | Admission Date, Offense Description <br> Sources: DCR Prison Data - Admissions                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| At what age do incarceration rates for violent offenses peak? How do these peak ages vary by specific types of violent offenses (e.g., assault, robbery, murder)?                                                                                         | Admission Date, Offense Description <br> Sources: DCR Prison Data - Admissions                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| At what age do incarceration rates for violent offenses peak? How do these peak ages vary by specific types of violent offenses (e.g., assault, robbery, murder)?                                                                                         | Admission Date, Offense Description <br> Sources: DCR Prison Data - Admissions                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| Survival analysis after release by age to determine if older individuals are less likely to be reincarcerated compared to younger individuals, by analyzing the time to reincarceration and comparing survival probabilities across different age groups. | Admission Date, Release Date <br> Sources: DCR Prison Data - Admissions, Releases, DCR Jail Data - Admissions, Releases                                                                                                                                              |

**Definitions**

-   Survival analysis is a statistical method used to study the time until an event happens, like reoffending after being released from prison. This analysis helps us understand patterns, such as whether older individuals take longer to reoffend or are less likely to reoffend compared to younger individuals.

## Parole

This section examines the demographics, offenses, and lengths of parole terms, as well as rates of violations and re-incarceration. It also examines regional differences in parole outcomes. The goal is to understand how parole impacts individuals and how variations in parole practices affect recidivism rates. This analysis will inform efforts to improve parole practices, support individuals on parole more effectively, and address disparities in outcomes across different regions.

| Research Questions                                                                                                          | Data Sources                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
|------------------------------------------|------------------------------|
| What are the demographic characteristics (age, gender, race, ethnicity) of individuals on parole?                           | DOB, gender, Race, Ethnicity <br> Sources: DCR Parole Data - Placements, Supervision                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| What are the most common offenses leading to parole placements?                                                             | Controlling Offense Code, Controlling Offense Description, Offense Type, Offense Severity/Degree <br> Sources: DCR Parole Data - Placements                                                                                                                          |
| What is the average length of parole terms?                                                                                 | Placement Date, Supervision End Date <br> Sources: DCR Parole Data - Placements, Supervision                                                                                                                                                                         |
| What is the rate of technical violations leading to parole revocations?                                                     | Type of Violation, Date of Violation <br> Sources: DCR Parole Data - Supervision                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| What proportion of individuals on parole have substance use disorders or mental health conditions?                          | Substance Abuse (SA) Screening/Assessment Scores, Known History of Drug Alcohol Abuse, Mental Health (MH) Screening/Assessment Scores, Known History of Mental Illness <br> Sources: DCR Parole Data - Placements                                                    |
| What are the housing statuses of individuals on parole?                                                                     | Housing Status <br> Sources: DCR Parole Data - Supervision                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| How often do individuals on parole receive program referrals?                                                               | Program Name, Program Type, Program Referral Date, Program Start Date, Program Termination/End Date, Type of Completion (successful, incomplete, removed, transferred to another facility, etc.) <br> Sources: DCR Parole Data - Program Referrals and Participation |
| What are the completion rates for parole terms?                                                                             | Placement Date, Supervision End Date, Type of Completion <br> Sources: DCR Parole Data - Supervision                                                                                                                                                                 |
| How do regional differences impact parole outcomes?                                                                         | County of Supervision <br> Sources: DCR Parole Data - Placements, Supervision                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| How many people on parole are re-incarcerated within a year of release?                                                     | Placement Date, Supervision End Date, Re-incarceration Date <br> Sources: DCR Parole Data - Placements, Releases                                                                                                                                                     |
| How many parole and probation officers are there per office? What is the average caseload size?                             | Policy Team/Stakeholder Data Needed                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| What is the rate of incarceration for violent crimes for people while on community supervision? I suspect this will be low. | Admission Type, Placement Date, Supervision End Date <br> Sources: DCR Prison Data - Admissions, DCR Parole Data - Placements, Supervision                                                                                                                           |
| What proportion of parole revocations are due to failure to meet financial obligations (e.g., fines, fees)?                 | ?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |

## Parole Revocations

This section explores the factors leading to parole revocations, examining the patterns of violations, the types of violations, and the consequences of these revocations.

| Research Questions                                                                         | Data Sources                                                                                                                                              |
|-----------------------------------|------------------------------------|
| What types of violations are most common among individuals whose parole is revoked?        | Type of Violation, Date of Violation <br> Sources: DCR Parole Data - Supervision, Revocations                                                             |
| What is the timeline of violations leading up to parole revocation?                        | Violation Dates, Violation Types <br> Sources: DCR Parole Data - Supervision, Revocations                                                                 |
| How do the rates of parole revocation vary by demographic factors?                         | Violation Rates, Demographic Information <br> Sources: DCR Parole Data - Supervision, Revocations                                                         |
| What proportion of parole revocations are due to technical violations versus new offenses? | Violation Type <br> Sources: DCR Parole Data - Supervision, Revocations                                                                                   |
| How quickly do people violate their supervision terms once placed on supervision?          | Violation Dates, Revocation Date <br> Sources: DCR Parole Data - Supervision, Revocations                                                                 |
| What are the patterns of re-incarceration following parole revocation?                     | Re-incarceration Dates, Violation Types <br> Sources: DCR Parole Data - Supervision, Revocations, Releases                                                |
| How do parole revocation rates vary across different regions or jurisdictions?             | Violation Rates, County of Supervision <br> Sources: DCR Parole Data - Supervision, Revocations                                                           |
| What role do substance use and mental health issues play in parole revocations?            | Substance Abuse (SA) Screening/Assessment Scores, Mental Health (MH) Screening/Assessment Scores <br> Sources: DCR Parole Data - Supervision, Revocations |

## Probation

Here, we will analyze the demographics, offenses, and lengths of probation terms, and examine rates of technical violations and re-incarceration. This section will also look into the housing statuses of individuals on probation, and the frequency of program referrals. The findings will help identify trends and challenges in probation management, and provide insights into how probation practices can be improved to support successful completion and reduce re-incarceration rates.

| Research Questions                                                                                    | Data Sources                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
|------------------------------------------|------------------------------|
| What are the demographic characteristics (age, gender, race, ethnicity) of individuals on probation?  | DOB, gender, Race, Ethnicity <br> Sources: AOC Data - Probation Supervision                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| What are the most common offenses leading to probation placements?                                    | Controlling Offense Code, Controlling Offense Description, Offense Type, Offense Severity/Degree <br> Sources: AOC Data - Probation Supervision                                                                                                               |
| What is the average length of probation terms?                                                        | Placement Date, Supervision End Date <br> Sources: AOC Data - Probation Supervision                                                                                                                                                                           |
| What is the rate of technical violations leading to probation revocations?                            | Type of Violation, Date of Violation <br> Sources: AOC Data - Probation Supervision                                                                                                                                                                           |
| What proportion of individuals on probation have substance use disorders or mental health conditions? | Substance Abuse (SA) Screening/Assessment Scores, Known History of Drug Alcohol Abuse, Mental Health (MH) Screening/Assessment Scores, Known History of Mental Illness <br> Sources: AOC Data - Probation Supervision                                         |
| What are the housing statuses of individuals on probation?                                            | Housing Status <br> Sources: AOC Data - Probation Supervision                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| How often do individuals on probation receive program referrals?                                      | Program Name, Program Type, Program Referral Date, Program Start Date, Program Termination/End Date, Type of Completion (successful, incomplete, removed, transferred to another facility, etc.) <br> Sources: AOC Data - Program Referrals and Participation |
| What are the completion rates for probation terms?                                                    | Placement Date, Supervision End Date, Type of Completion <br> Sources: AOC Data - Probation Supervision                                                                                                                                                       |
| How do regional differences impact probation outcomes?                                                | County of Supervision <br> Sources: AOC Data - Probation Supervision                                                                                                                                                                                          |

## Behavioral Health

This section examines the prevalence of behavioral health issues, such as mental health disorders and substance use disorders, among individuals in the criminal justice system. The aim is to provide an overview of how these issues affect the population and inform potential areas for intervention.

| Research Questions                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Data Sources                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
|------------------------------------------|------------------------------|
| Assess and map the treatments and services available in prisons and jails as well as the community, including substance use and mental health treatment, cognitive behavioral programming, housing, and job assistance.                                                                  | Policy Team/Stakeholder Data Needed                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| Map EM responses for suspected overdoses                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | [EMS Rsponses for Suspected Overdoses](https://dhhr.wv.gov/office-of-drug-control-policy/datadashboard/Pages/default.aspx)                                                                                           |
| Map ER visits for overdoses                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | [ER Visits for Overdoses](https://dhhr.wv.gov/office-of-drug-control-policy/datadashboard/Pages/default.aspx)                                                                                                        |
| Map of overdoses                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | [Current Fatalities](https://dhhr.wv.gov/office-of-drug-control-policy/datadashboard/Pages/default.aspx)                                                                                                             |
| What is the prevalence of mental health disorders and substance use disorders among the CJ population?                                                                                                                                                                                   | Mental Health (MH) Screening/Assessment Scores, Known History of Mental Illness <br> Sources: DCR Data - Assessments (limited availability)                                                                          |
| How do BH conditions impact recidivism rates?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Substance Abuse (SA) Screening/Assessment Scores, Known History of Drug Alcohol Abuse, Re-incarceration Date, Mental Health (MH) Screening/Assessment Scores, Known History of Mental Illness <br> Sources: DCR Data |
| How do mental health screening and assessments vary across correctional facilities?                                                                                                                                                                                                      | Location, Mental Health (MH) Screening/Assessment Scores, Substance Abuse (SA) Screening/Assessment Scores <br> Sources: DCR Data - Assessments                                                                      |
| Are people with BH conditions less successful on probation and parole?                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Substance Abuse (SA) Screening/Assessment Scores, Mental Health (MH) Screening/Assessment Scores <br> Sources: DCR Data - Supervision                                                                                |
| Survival analysis after release by age to determine if older individuals are less likely to be reincarcerated while on community supervision compared to younger individuals, by analyzing the time to reincarceration and comparing survival probabilities across different age groups. | Admission Date, Release Date <br> Sources: DCR Prison Data - Admissions, Releases, DCR Jail Data - Admissions, Releases, DCR Probation/Parole Data - Admissions, Releases, Revocations                               |
| How do BH flags vary by race, ethnicity, and gender?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | Substance Abuse (SA) Screening/Assessment Scores, Known History of Drug Alcohol Abuse, Mental Health (MH) Screening/Assessment Scores, Known History of Mental Illness <br> Sources: DCR Data                        |
| How many people in the facilities have OUD? How many are on MAT?                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Policy Team/Stakeholder Data Needed                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| Which treatment services in the community have waitlists? If so, how long is the waitlist?                                                                                                                                                                                               | Policy Team/Stakeholder Data Needed                                                                                                                                                                                  |

## Recidivism

The recidivism analysis aims to assess overall rates of re-incarceration, the impact of pretrial detention and bail decisions, and the role of reentry programs in reducing recidivism. By understanding these factors, we can identify barriers to successful reintegration and develop strategies to improve reentry support. This section is vital for addressing the root causes of recidivism and designing interventions that enhance long-term success for individuals leaving the criminal justice system.

| Research Questions                                                                                                                                                               | Data Sources                                                                                                                                                 |
|------------------------------------------|------------------------------|
| What are the overall recidivism rates for different offense types and demographic groups?                                                                                        | Admission Date, Release Date, Re-incarceration Date <br> Sources: DCR and AOC Data                                                                           |
| What are the patterns of recidivism among individuals with BH conditions?                                                                                                        | Substance Abuse (SA) Screening/Assessment Scores, Known History of Drug Alcohol Abuse <br> Sources: DCR Data                                                 |
| How do recidivism rates vary by county? What makes counties with high recidivism rates unique? (Lack of services, parole offices ar far away, high overdose fatality rate, etc.) | Admission Date, Release Date, Supervising County, Release County <br> Sources: DCR Prison, Jail, Probation, Parole Data, Policy Team/Stakeholder Data Needed |

## Day Report Centers

This section examines the role and usage of day reporting centers within the criminal justice system. It covers the types of services offered, the demographics of individuals attending these centers, and overall trends in their utilization. The goal is to understand how day reporting centers function, who they serve, and their general impact on managing individuals under supervision.

| Research Questions                                                                                               | Data Sources                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|------------------------------------------|------------------------------|
| What are the demographic characteristics (age, gender, race, ethnicity) of individuals using Day Report Centers? | DOB, Gender, Race, Ethnicity <br> Sources: DCR Data - Program Referrals and Participation                                                                                                                                                                               |
| What services are most frequently utilized at Day Report Centers?                                                | Program Name, Program Type, Program Referral Date, Program Start Date, Program Termination/End Date, Type of Completion (successful, incomplete, removed, transferred to another facility, etc.) <br> Sources: DCR Data - Program Referrals and Participation           |
| What are the completion rates for programs offered at Day Report Centers?                                        | Program Name, Program Type, Program Start Date, Program Termination/End Date, Type of Completion (successful, incomplete, removed, transferred to another facility, etc.) <br> Sources: DCR Data - Program Referrals and Participation                                  |
| What are the recidivism rates for people who stayed at Day Report Centers?                                       | Program Name, Program Type, Program Start Date, Program Termination/End Date, Type of Completion (successful, incomplete, removed, transferred to another facility, etc.), Re-incarceration Date <br> Sources: DCR Data - Program Referrals and Participation, Releases |
| What are the patterns of attendance and participation in Day Report Center programs?                             | Program Name, Program Type, Program Referral Date, Program Start Date, Program Termination/End Date, Attendance Records <br> Sources: DCR Data - Program Referrals and Participation                                                                                    |
| What is the incidence of substance use disorders among individuals at Day Report Centers?                        | Substance Abuse (SA) Screening/Assessment Scores, Known History of Drug Alcohol Abuse <br> Sources: DCR Data - Program Referrals and Participation                                                                                                                      |

## Disparities

This explores disparities within the criminal justice system, focusing on how various factors such as race, ethnicity, and Gender status influence criminal justice outcomes. Identifying and understanding these disparities is crucial for developing fair and equitable policies.

| Research Questions                                                                          | Data Sources                                                                                            |
|------------------------------------------|------------------------------|
| What are the disparities in bail amounts set for different demographic groups?              | Bail Amount, Demographic Information <br> Sources: AOC Data                                             |
| How do sentencing outcomes differ by race, ethnicity, and gender?                           | Sentence Length, Demographic Information <br> Sources: DCR Prison Data - Admissions                     |
| What are the disparities in pretrial detention rates across different demographic groups?   | Pretrial Detention Status, Demographic Information <br> Sources: AOC Data, DCR Jail Data - Admissions   |
| How do recidivism rates vary by race, ethnicity, and gender?                                | Recidivism Rates, Demographic Information <br> Sources: DCR Data, AOC Data                              |
| What are the systemic factors contributing to disparities in sentencing and bail decisions? | Systemic Factors (e.g., judicial practices, policies) <br> Sources: Policy Team/Stakeholder Data Needed |

## Parole Eligibility

This section examines the criteria and trends related to parole eligibility, including how eligibility is determined and the general process involved. Many people are kept in prison past their parole eligibility date across the country.

| Research Questions                                                                                                                                                                             | Data Sources                                                                                                             |
|------------------------------------------|------------------------------|
| What are the demographic characteristics (age, gender, race, ethnicity) of individuals eligible for parole?                                                                                    | DOB, gender, Race, Ethnicity <br> Sources: TBD Parole Board Decisions, DCR Prison Data                                   |
| How do parole eligibility dates impact prison populations?                                                                                                                                     | Admission Date, Release Date, Parole Eligibility Date <br> Sources: DCR Prison Data - Admissions, Releases               |
| What are the most common reasons for parole denials?                                                                                                                                           | Parole Hearing Outcome <br> Sources: TBD Parole Board Decisions                                                          |
| How do risk assessment scores influence parole eligibility decisions?                                                                                                                          | Risk Assessment Scores <br> Sources: TBD Parole Board Decisions                                                          |
| What is the average time to parole eligibility for different offense types? Demographics?                                                                                                      | Admission Date, Parole Eligibility Date <br> Sources: TBD Parole Board Decisions, DCR Prison Data - Admissions, Releases |
| What proportion of eligible individuals are granted parole at their first hearing?                                                                                                             | Parole Hearing Outcome <br> Sources: TBD Parole Board Decisions                                                          |
| How do parole eligibility decisions vary across different regions or jurisdictions?                                                                                                            | County of Supervision <br> Sources: TBD Parole Board Decisions                                                           |
| What are the patterns of reoffending among individuals granted parole?                                                                                                                         | Re-incarceration Date <br> Sources: TBD Parole Board Decisions, DCR Jail/Prison Data                                     |
| Are there any disparities in the timing of parole release? Are White people more likely to be granted parole within one year of parole eligibility compared to other racial and ethnic groups? | Parole Hearing Outcome, Re-incarceration Date <br> Sources: TBD Parole Board Decisions, DCR Jail/Prison Data             |

<br>
